zEImplementation of the GDB 'explore' command using the GDB Python API.
Explorerz-Internal class which invokes other explorers.c
r*n^d
s~d	|
len)
exprZ
lengthZ
guard
,/usr/share/gdb/python/gdb/command/explore.py
guard_expr*
Explorer.guard_exprc
Main function to explore an expression value.
        Arguments:
            expr: The expression string that is being explored.
            value: The gdb.Value value of the expression.
            is_child: Boolean value to indicate if the expression is a child.
                      An expression is a child if it is derived from the main
                      expression entered by the user.  For example, if the user
                      entered an expression which evaluates to a struct, then
                      when exploring the fields of the struct, is_child is set
                      to True internally.
        Returns:
            No return value.
        
*Explorer for type '%s' not yet available.
type
coder
type_code_to_explorer_map
explore_expr
print
str)
value
is_child
	type_code
explorer_classr
Explorer.explore_exprc
Main function to explore a data type.
        Arguments:
            name: The string representing the path to the data type being
                  explored.
            datatype: The gdb.Type value of the data type being explored.
            is_child: Boolean value to indicate if the name is a child.
                      A name is a child if it is derived from the main name
                      entered by the user.  For example, if the user entered
                      the name of struct type, then when exploring the fields
                      of the struct, is_child is set to True internally.
        Returns:
            No return value.
        r
explore_typer
name
datatyper
Explorer.explore_typec
Initializes the Explorer environment.
        This function should be invoked before starting any exploration.  If
        invoked before an exploration, it need not be invoked for subsequent
        explorations.
        N)
TYPE_CODE_CHAR

TYPE_CODE_ARRAY
ArrayExplorerr
init_envx
Explorer.init_envc
Checks whether a type is a scalar type.
        A type is a scalar type of its type is
            gdb.TYPE_CODE_CHAR or
            gdb.TYPE_CODE_INT or
            gdb.TYPE_CODE_BOOL or
            gdb.TYPE_CODE_FLT or
            gdb.TYPE_CODE_VOID or
            gdb.TYPE_CODE_ENUM.
        Arguments:
            type: The type to be checked.
        Returns:
            'True' if 'type' is a scalar type. 'False' otherwise.
        )
_SCALAR_TYPE_LIST)
is_scalar_type
Explorer.is_scalar_typec
A utility function which prints that the current exploration session
        is returning to the parent value. Useful when exploring values.
        z
Returning to parent value...
return_to_parent_value
Explorer.return_to_parent_valuec
A utility function which prompts the user to press the 'enter' key
        so that the exploration session can shift back to the parent value.
        Useful when exploring values.
        z(
Press enter to return to parent value: N
	raw_inputr
return_to_parent_value_prompt
z&Explorer.return_to_parent_value_promptc
A utility function which prints that the current exploration session
        is returning to the enclosing type.  Useful when exploring types.
        z 
Returning to enclosing type...

z!Explorer.return_to_enclosing_typec
A utility function which prompts the user to press the 'enter' key
        so that the exploration session can shift back to the enclosing type.
        Useful when exploring types.
        z*

        information.
        z$'%s' is a scalar value of type '%s'.z

TypedefExplorer.explore_typeNrA
ExploreUtilszEInternal class which provides utilities for the main command classes.c
Utility to check if adequate number of arguments are passed to an
        explore command.
        Arguments:
            name: The name of the explore command.
            arg_str: The argument string passed to the explore command.
        Returns:
            True if adequate arguments are passed, false otherwise.
        Raises:
            gdb.GdbError if adequate arguments are not passed.
        r
z!ERROR: '%s' requires an argument.FTN)
GdbError)
arg_strr
check_argsl
ExploreUtils.check_argsc
A utility function to deduce the gdb.Type value from a string
        representing the type.
        Arguments:
            type_str: The type string from which the gdb.Type value should be
                      deduced.
        Returns:
            The deduced gdb.Type value if possible, None otherwise.
        z
(%s *)0N)
parse_and_evalr
RuntimeErrorZ
lookup_type)
type_strr
get_type_from_str
ExploreUtils.get_type_from_strc
A utility function to deduce the gdb.Value value from a string
        representing the value.
        Arguments:
            value_str: The value string from which the gdb.Value value should
                       be deduced.
        Returns:
            The deduced gdb.Value value if possible, None otherwise.
        N)
Z	value_strr
get_value_from_str
ExploreUtils.get_value_from_strN)
ExploreCommanda	
Explore a value or a type valid in the current context.
    Usage: explore ARG
    - ARG is either a valid expression or a type name.
    - At any stage of exploration, hit the return key (instead of a
    choice, if any) to return to the enclosing type or value.c
exploreT)
command_class
prefix)
superrs
__init__r#
COMMAND_DATA
self
	__class__r
ExploreCommand.__init__c
FzG'%s' neither evaluates to a value nor is a type in the current context.)	rk
from_ttyr
invoke
ExploreCommand.invoke
__classcell__r
ExploreValueCommandz
Explore value of an expression valid in the current context.
    Usage: explore value ARG
    - ARG is a valid expression.
    - At any stage of exploration, hit the return key (instead of a
    choice, if any) to return to the enclosing value.c
explore value
ExploreValueCommand.__init__c
Fz: '%s' does not evaluate to a value in the current context.)
ExploreValueCommand.invoker
ExploreTypeCommandz
Explore a type or the type of an expression.
    Usage: explore type ARG
    - ARG is a valid expression or a type name.
    - At any stage of exploration, hit the return key (instead of a
    choice, if any) to return to the enclosing type.c
explore typer
ExploreTypeCommand.__init__c
'%s' is of type '%s'.z3'%s' is not a type or value in the current context.)
ExploreTypeCommand.invoker

inputr8
